Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. 15 Years of Forking 267 upvotes, 57 comments
  2. Douglas Lenat's Automated Mathematician Source Code 58 upvotes, 9 comments
  3. The curious case of retro demo scene graphics 299 upvotes, 74 comments
  4. Copilot edited an ad into my PR 1206 upvotes, 345 comments
  5. Hardware Image Compression 55 upvotes, 10 comments
  6. Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman Equation: Reinforcement Learning and Diffusion Models 120 upvotes, 33 comments
  7. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 637 upvotes, 238 comments
  8. Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI 140 upvotes, 49 comments
  9. VHDL's Crown Jewel 117 upvotes, 40 comments
  10. How to turn anything into a router 271 upvotes, 112 comments
  11. I use Excalidraw to manage my diagrams for my blog 206 upvotes, 87 comments
  12. Ninja is a small build system with a focus on speed 71 upvotes, 25 comments
  13. Comprehensive C++ Hashmap Benchmarks (2022) 41 upvotes, 13 comments
  14. Philly courts will ban all smart eyeglasses starting next week 369 upvotes, 182 comments
  15. Build123d: A Python CAD programming library 41 upvotes, 14 comments
  16. "Over 1.5 million GitHub PRs have had ads injected into them by Copilot" 226 upvotes, 116 comments
  17. FTC Action Against Match and OkCupid for Deceiving Users, Sharing Personal Data 63 upvotes, 26 comments
  18. ChatGPT won't let you type until Cloudflare reads your React state 883 upvotes, 576 comments
  19. Parrots pack twice as many neurons as primate brains of the same mass 184 upvotes, 108 comments
  20. OCR For construction documents does not work, we fixed it 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  21. CodingFont: A game to help you pick a coding font 64 upvotes, 38 comments
  22. From Proxmox to FreeBSD and Sylve in Our Office Lab 25 upvotes, 13 comments
  23. Spring Boot Done Right: Lessons from a 400-Module Codebase 67 upvotes, 49 comments
  24. 72% of the dollar's purchasing power was destroyed in just four episodes 137 upvotes, 123 comments
  25. In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? 45 upvotes, 35 comments
  26. C++26 is done: ISO C++ standards meeting Trip Report 292 upvotes, 318 comments
  27. Cherri – programming language that compiles directly to a Apple Shortuct 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. Bitwarden Integrates with OneCLI Agent Vault 26 upvotes, 19 comments
  29. I am definitely missing the pre-AI writing era 94 upvotes, 93 comments
  30. My MacBook keyboard is broken and it's insanely expensive to fix 324 upvotes, 400 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 31 March 2026. Source here.
